the ruin of Ksatriyas; if yellow, calamity to Vaisyas (trading

nity). Still if he is glossy, he will prove auspicious to all the

Being blood-red in summer the Sun causes danger to the
people; in the rainy season he causes drought, being dark in
appearance; and being yellowish in the dewy season he leads to
the outbreak of diseases in the near future.

The Sun's Transit III 29
If the Sun's disc is cut by the rainbow, it bodes evil to the
ruler of the country. If the Sun looks quite bright in the rainy
season, it betokens immediate rain. Similarly, if the Sun be of the
colour of the Sirisa flower in monsoon, there will be immediate
rain. If, on the other hand, the Sun has the colour of the pea-
